topic: Percentage increases and cuts in a budget
title: The Schofield family in trouble
instruction: The Schofield family face financial difficulties. Answer these questions.
context: The Schofields' monthly income was £2,600. Mr Schofield's hours were cut — income fell by 15%. Meanwhile: rent increased by 10% from £800 | energy bills increased by 20% from £150 | food costs increased by 8% from £350.

questions.question: Mr Schofield's 'hours were cut'. What does this mean for their income?
options: He works longer hours for less pay
He works fewer hours and earns less
questions.answer: He works fewer hours and earns less

questions.question: Bills and rent 'increased'. What is another word for increased?
The family need to 'prioritise' their spending. What does prioritise mean?
options: Cut all spending by the same amount

questions.question: Income fell by 15% from £2,600. What is the new monthly income?
Rent increased by 10% from £800. What is the new rent?
Energy bills increased by 20% from £150. What is the new bill?
